description_long: This object is a historical-style cannon mounted on a wooden carriage, featuring a large black metal barrel with a flared muzzle and a prominent reinforcing ring near the breech. The carriage is constructed from light-colored wood, with four solid wooden wheels and metal axles for mobility. Two curved metal support bars run along the sides, connecting the front and rear of the carriage, likely for structural reinforcement and maneuvering. The cannon barrel is secured to the carriage with visible trunnions and a rear knob for aiming adjustments. The overall design is robust, with the wood showing a natural grain and the metal surfaces appearing matte and slightly textured. This type of vehicle would have been used for combat purposes, typically in historical or ceremonial urban settings.
description: A historical black metal cannon mounted on a wooden wheeled carriage with metal support bars and a robust, detailed design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle of the cannon, the wooden carriage, and the symmetrical arrangement of the support bars.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the length of the cannon barrel, the wooden wheels, and the side support bar.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the cannon barrel, the rear knob, and the full width of the carriage.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the opposite support bar and wheel arrangement.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One large fixed cannon barrel mounted centrally on the wooden carriage; no additional mounts visible.
attribute_armor_plating: None visible; the object consists of exposed metal and wood with no additional armor plating.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; the cannon is finished in solid black and natural wood colors.
